symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent falls (three in one month) with recently unsteady, wide-based, slow gait

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recurrent falls pattern with gait deterioration defines the syndrome and mandates a multidomain falls assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to bear weight or self-recover from the floor after the fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult fractured neck of femur or pelvic/vertebral fracture
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to weight-bear after a fall in a frail elder is a red flag for occult fracture that cannot be excluded via telehealth.
Being stranded and unable to rise while alone drives rhabdomyolysis, pressure injury and hypothermia risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Proximal lower-limb weakness — legs 'give way', difficulty rising from chairs and floor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Proximal weakness impairs balance recovery and the ability to rise, a core contributor to the falls.

symptoms.symptom_name: No dizziness, lightheadedness or visual greying on standing before the fall (fall occurred on turning)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of presyncope and a turning-related mechanical mechanism lower the probability of an orthostatic/syncopal cause, but do not exclude it without postural BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: No head strike, no loss of consciousness, no severe hip/groin pain reported

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult fractured neck of femur or pelvic/vertebral fracture
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of severe pain is reassuring but does NOT exclude a hip fracture, which can present with modest pain in the frail elderly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sedating and hypotensive polypharmacy — recently commenced night-time benzodiazepine plus two antihypertensives

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recently started benzodiazepine and dual antihypertensives are a classic Beers-criteria modifiable contributor to instability and falls.

symptoms.symptom_name: No hip shortening, no external rotation, no pain on passive internal rotation of either hip

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician hip examination that excluded a fractured neck of femur — the AI Doctor cannot perform this and so cannot exclude the fracture remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical proximal lower-limb weakness (power 4/5 hip flexors and knee extensors), intact sensation, normal reflexes, no focal deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician neurological examination — bilateral symmetrical weakness without upper-motor-neuron signs argues against stroke; unavailable to the AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Timed-Up-and-Go test (well over 12 seconds) on assisted standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective gait-and-balance impairment confirming high falls risk — a hands-on assessment the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs — BP 145/85, HR 82, Temp 36.6, RR 16, SpO2 98%

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute sepsis or cardiorespiratory decompensation as fall cause
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician-measured vitals are stable and afebrile, arguing against an acute septic/cardiorespiratory precipitant; the AI Doctor cannot measure these and must not assume them.
